text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Everwood, there lived a peculiar man named Bartholomew. Bartholomew had a pet raven named Whisper who could talk. One peculiar afternoon, while browsing through an antique shop cluttered with oddities, Bartholomew stumbled upon a clock that glowed soft green lights. It had a single antique bronze statue hanging on the floor beside its pendulum. That night, Whisper cooed instructions for brewing a batch of herbs meant to soothe Bartholomew's insomnia.

Bartholomew followed Whisper’s instructions, which turned out to be a series of cryptic bird chirps followed by whimsical tales about moonlit nights filled with stars dancing away his worries. By morning light, he found himself wide awake—thankful, actually.

The clock became an obsession though. Each night, the owl-like creature on the statue would owl around Bartholomew a prophecy whispered in old birdese. One night it
